task_completion.description: Configure Autopsy's Central Repository to use a SQLite database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Autopsy Options or Settings window open, showing the 'Central Repository' tab with the SQLite database option selected and configured.

task_completion.description: Create or open the 'Operation_Alpha' case and add the suspect_alpha.dd data source.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Autopsy UI showing a case named 'Operation_Alpha' (or 'INV-01') with the data source 'suspect_alpha.dd' loaded in the tree view.

task_completion.description: Create or open the 'Operation_Beta' case and add the suspect_beta.dd data source.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Autopsy UI showing a case named 'Operation_Beta' (or 'INV-02') with the data source 'suspect_beta.dd' loaded in the tree view.

task_completion.description: Use the Correlation Engine to identify overlapping files by MD5 hash between the two cases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Autopsy UI showing the 'Other Occurrences' tab, Central Repository results, or Correlation Engine output, displaying a matching MD5 hash linking files from both the Alpha and Beta cases.

task_completion.description: Write the intelligence brief to /home/ga/Reports/cross_case_intelligence.txt with the specified format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window showing the contents of '/home/ga/Reports/cross_case_intelligence.txt' containing 'CENTRAL_REPO_CONFIGURED: YES', 'TOTAL_OVERLAPPING_FILES: [count]', and 'MATCH: <md5_hash> | <filename_in_beta> | <filename_in_alpha>'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Autopsy GUI to configure the Central Repository and find the correlation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the Autopsy graphical interface, rather than bypassing it using command-line SQLite queries or Sleuthkit terminal tools.

integrity.description: The agent discovered the MD5 hash through the Autopsy application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The MD5 hash written in the final report should match the hash visible in the Autopsy correlation results on screen. The agent should not be seen opening '/tmp/shared_md5.txt' to cheat.

integrity.description: The agent actually created and saved the report file.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, vim, gedit) or a terminal command (like echo/cat) is visibly used to write the required text to the exact path '/home/ga/Reports/cross_case_intelligence.txt'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected report file must be located exactly at '/home/ga/Reports/cross_case_intelligence.txt'. The shared MD5 hash is randomized per run and stored in '/tmp/shared_md5.txt' for backend verification, but the agent must find it via Autopsy's Central Repository. Verify that the MD5 hash in the final report matches the one found in the Autopsy GUI.
